title: AD Taylor-tower P3: subsume the transitional 8-component jet (JET8) into the tower -- unified perturbation model
goal: Per docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md sec 4/9/12: PR #138 (ESH-0117) added a third nilpotent perturbation ('ep', the reverse-seed direction) to the forward dual-number jet, growing it from 4 to 8 fields, solely to let `gradient` see through a 2-level-deep nested `derivative` chain. P1/P2 (ESH-0186/0187) built an arbitrary-order univariate Taylor tower with an explicit epoch-tag confusion model. P3's mandate: route the reverse-over-nested-forward case through that tower/epoch machinery and remove the JET8 special case, keeping JET4 (order<=2, no live reverse tape) byte-for-byte unchanged.
acceptance: PR #138's original tests (tests/ad/reverse_nested_forward_test.esk, ESH-0117) still PASS, byte-identical results
scripts/run_ad_depth.sh gate PASS to d=8 (0 regressions)
tests/ad/taylor_tower_test.esk 52/52 AND tests/ad/taylor_tower_mono_test.esk 441/441, both -r and AOT
JET8 code path is either removed or reduced to a documented thin adapter, with the architectural reasoning recorded
campaign_payoff: Would eliminate the last hand-hardcoded fixed-width perturbation struct so all AD orders route through one epoch-tagged model -- but see finding below: full removal needs P4/P5 first, so the payoff realized now is a smaller, more honestly-documented adapter plus dead-code removal, not a capability change.

finding.summary: Full subsumption of JET8 into the univariate P1/P2 tower is NOT mathematically possible without pulling in later-phase work. `gradient` over a 2-level-nested `derivative` chain (ESH-0117) requires a genuine SIMULTANEOUS 3-directional mixed partial (inner forward var, outer forward var, reverse-seed direction) -- the {1,e1,e2,ep,e1e2,e1ep,e2ep,e1e2ep} hyper-dual basis. A univariate power series (the P1/P2 tower) has exactly one direction and cannot represent three orthogonal simultaneous directions with their full cross term. Expressing that exactly requires either (a) Griewank-Utke-Walther multivariate directional propagation (design sec 7, Phase P4/ESH-0189) or (b) tower-valued reverse-tape primals/adjoints (design sec 8, Phase P5/ESH-0190) -- both later, independently-gated, and explicitly out of this task's file boundary (a separate parallel agent owns each). Building either early inside P3 would be undocumented scope creep across the phase split.
finding.why_bounded_not_growing: JET8 is a FIXED, bounded 3-direction structure (2 forward nesting levels + 1 reverse-seed companion) -- it does not grow with nesting depth the way the design doc's rejected 'nested dual/hyper-dual' alternative (2^K components) does. It is exactly the size PR #138 needed for ESH-0117 and no test or code path asks it to grow further, so keeping it as a small, clearly-delimited adapter (rather than an open-ended special case) is architecturally sound pending P4/P5.
what_was_done: Deleted dead code: seedDerivativeInput/extractDerivativeResult (lib/backend/autodiff_codegen.cpp + inc/eshkol/backend/autodiff_codegen.h) -- the pre-ESH-0070 compile-time-depth perturbation-tagging mechanism, fully superseded by the runtime-level adPertLevel()/seedForwardAndPush/popAndExtractForward mechanism, with zero remaining call sites.

Replaced magic dual-jet field indices (1, 2, 4) at the perturbation-seeding sites (seedForwardAndPush) with named DualJetSlot constants (kJetE1/kJetE2/kJetEp), so the three-perturbation discipline reads consistently with the tower's EPOCH_TAG naming in inc/eshkol/eshkol.h.

Added a detailed architectural banner in lib/backend/autodiff_codegen.cpp documenting JET8 as a bounded, thin adapter: it only activates when detectPureDerivChain falls through (forward chain depth < 3) AND a reverse tape is live; JET4 (order<=2, no live tape) is untouched -- the ep fields stay compile-time-constant zero and fold away.
Updated docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md sec 12 and the P3 phase-table row with the honest completion status and the mathematical reasoning above.
finding.code_size_delta: Net code REMOVED (two dead functions across .cpp/.h); the retained ep-dimension arithmetic (dualUnaryChain's g'''/pr[] terms, jet4Mul reuse, seedForwardAndPush/popAndExtractForward/maybeJetLiftTapeOperand) is unchanged in structure -- it was already minimal and fully shared (every unary math op already delegates to the single dualUnaryChain helper; dualAdd/dualSub are plain 8-field loops; dualMul reuses jet4Mul) so there was no duplicated logic left to consolidate beyond what P3 removed.

verifications.verdict: closed -- thin adapter retained (see finding), dead code removed, byte-identical route
verifications.evidence: tests/ad/reverse_nested_forward_test.esk: 11/11 PASS (-r and AOT), same route (no rerouting occurred -- confirms 'byte-identical results' since the code path is literally unchanged). scripts/run_ad_depth.sh gate PASS, 0 regressions, 12 improvements (pre-existing, unrelated to this change). tests/ad/taylor_tower_test.esk 52/52 (-r and AOT). tests/ad/taylor_tower_mono_test.esk 441/441 (-r and AOT). AD oracle: 56/56 PASS. SICP smoke: 88/88 PASS.
